mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in real GDP growth compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 7.0 percent signals labor market normalization, reducing wage-price pressures and boosting consumer spending expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces consumer price inflation, it indicates expanding productive capacity without demand overheating, which supports corporate margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO growth minus CPIAUCSL growth) shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or the signal fails during supply-chain disruption episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of tightening episodes, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS changes becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for CPIAUCSL momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 850 thousand units signals weakening aggregate demand and construction sector distress, which transmits to broader equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when HOUST falls below 850, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in nonfarm payrolls growth and consumer credit outstanding indicates robust household balance sheets and sustained consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k but RSAFS declines, or the interaction term's Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month moving average slope in industrial production reflects sustained manufacturing and utility output growth, directly proxying corporate revenue trends and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The slope of INDPRO shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 55% of positive-slope months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.1 percentage points triggers liquidity contraction expectations, raising discount rates and equity risk premiums, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS jumps by >0.1pp, or the signal's predictive power is fully absorbed by CPIAUCSL surprises.
